sourceMovie.title: Samaritan
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Samaritan (2022) stuck because it commits to its science fiction premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Julius Avery's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The science fiction and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Demolition Man
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Samaritan clicked for you, Demolition Man (1993) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and crime, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Samaritan?
faq.answer: Try Demolition Man, Code 8, Code 8 Part II—each shares DNA with Samaritan while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Samaritan?
faq.answer: Samaritan (2022) is best described as Science Fiction, Crime, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Samaritan part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Samaritan Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
